title: Quadlet: add network support
body:
Support `.network` files to create a systemd service that runs `podman network create`
Support networks with `.network` suffix in Container and Kube to link with Quadlet created networks 
Add E2E Tests

Signed-off-by: Ygal Blum <ygal.blum@gmail.com>

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
Yes

```release-note
Quadlet: allow users to create podman networks using quadlet and link them to services created via .container and .kube files
```

Resolves: #16596

comments.body: We had a previous discussion about exposing network creation via systemd unit file, with some objections raised... I'll try and find the issue, because I can't remember why we didn't end up doing it.

> We had a previous discussion about exposing network creation via systemd unit file, with some objections raised... I'll try and find the issue, because I can't remember why we didn't end up doing it.

Thinking about it, I think a major issue is the following scenario:
1. First time the service runs, the network is created
2. The machine reboots
3. The service tries to run again and fails because the the network already exists

A similar issue may occur on any persistent object. I can see that to avoid this issue for volumes @alexlarsson added an `--ignore` argument to `podman volume create` and used in Quadlet's `.volume` service. So, I think I should do something similar here.
@Luap99 can you please take a look to help me stop the vicious rebase cycle :)
We want added docs for the .network type in `docs/source/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.md`

> We want added docs for the .network type in `docs/source/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.md`

Done

I will approve and wait for @edsantiago for the best way to maintain man pages changes.

/lgtm
